text.title: Django BaseModel Antipatterns
text.summary: Common mistakes when implementing or consuming BaseModel, with emphasis on LLM-specific failure modes.

antipattern.description: Exposing auto-increment id in DRF serializers or URL patterns instead of uid.
antipattern.reason: Leaks table size, enables enumeration attacks, and couples public API to internal DB layout. Always use uid in Meta.fields and set lookup_field = 'uid'.

antipattern.description: Overriding Meta in a child model without inheriting BaseModel.Meta (class Meta(BaseModel.Meta)).
antipattern.reason: Silently loses the base ordering and get_latest_by; discovered only when querysets return unexpected order in production.

antipattern.description: Using default=uuid.uuid4() (called once at import time) instead of default=uuid.uuid4 (callable).
antipattern.reason: All rows created after the first migration get the same UUID. Caught only by a test that creates two rows and checks uid uniqueness.

antipattern.description: Applying BaseModel to M2M through-tables and intermediate models.
antipattern.reason: Through-tables are link entities, not domain entities. Extra UUID + index balloons index count for no benefit.

antipattern.description: Squashing migrations without verifying the UUID unique=True constraint is preserved.
antipattern.reason: Auto-squash can drop and re-add constraints without backfilling — corrupts production data. Never auto-squash; always review the generated migration.

example.code: # Missing composite index — full-table scan on common query class Order(BaseModel): status = models.CharField(max_length=20) user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.PROTECT)
